skill.name: microsoft-teams-automation
skill.description: Automate Microsoft Teams tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): send messages, manage channels, create meetings, handle chats, and search messages. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Microsoft Teams operations through Composio's Rube MCP integration for messaging, meetings, and channel management.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 67 potential security issues, all of which are false positives from scanning markdown documentation. The flagged 'external_commands' patterns are tool name examples in code blocks (e.g., MICROSOFT_TEAMS_TEAMS_LIST), not executable shell commands. The 'hardcoded URL' is legitimate documentation of the required Rube MCP server endpoint (https://rube.app/mcp). The skill is a legitimate Microsoft Teams automation guide with no actual executable code or security risks.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation
low_findings.description: The file contains a hardcoded URL (https://rube.app/mcp) at line 22. This is legitimate documentation showing users where to connect the Rube MCP server, not executing network requests.

content.user_title: Automate Microsoft Teams with Claude Code
content.value_statement: Stop switching between applications to manage Microsoft Teams. This skill helps you send messages, create meetings, and manage channels directly from Claude Code using natural language commands.
actual_capabilities: Send messages to Teams channels and chats using natural language
Create and manage Teams meetings with participants
List and manage Teams, channels, and members
Search messages across chats and channels using KQL syntax
Resolve team, channel, and user IDs automatically through workflows
Handle pagination and rate limiting for Microsoft Graph API
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and Microsoft Teams OAuth authentication
Cannot create calendar-linked meetings (only standalone meetings)
Message search has 30-60 second delay for newly posted messages
Some operations require specific Microsoft Graph permissions or admin consent
Messages larger than 28KB must be split into multiple parts

use_cases.title: Send Status Updates to Teams
use_cases.description: Project managers can automatically post daily status updates to specific Teams channels without leaving their development environment.

use_cases.title: Schedule Team Meetings
use_cases.description: Team leads can create Teams meetings by describing participants and time in natural language, with automatic user ID resolution.

use_cases.title: Search Conversation History
use_cases.description: Developers can quickly find specific messages or decisions discussed in Teams chats and channels using keyword search.

prompt_templates.title: Send a Channel Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Send a message to the 'Project Updates' channel in the 'Engineering' team saying 'The deployment was successful and is now live in production.'
prompt_templates.scenario: Quickly broadcast deployment status to your team

prompt_templates.title: Create a Team Meeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Teams meeting titled 'Sprint Review' for tomorrow at 2pm UTC, ending at 3pm UTC, with participants John (john@example.com) and Sarah (sarah@example.com).
prompt_templates.scenario: Schedule recurring or one-time team meetings

prompt_templates.title: Search for Messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Search all Teams messages from 'alex@company.com' sent in the last week containing 'deployment'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Find specific conversations or decisions in chat history

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Chats
prompt_templates.prompt: List all my recent Teams chats and show me the last message from each chat.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quickly review ongoing conversations across multiple chats

output_examples.input: Send 'Hello team!' to the General channel in the Marketing team
output_examples.output: Successfully posted message 'Hello team!' to the General channel in the Marketing team. Message ID: 1234567890

output_examples.input: Create a meeting called 'Weekly Sync' for Monday 10am to 11am with the product team
output_examples.output: Created Teams meeting 'Weekly Sync' for Monday from 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM UTC. Meeting link: https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/abc123

output_examples.input: Find all messages from Sarah containing 'deadline'
output_examples.output: Found 3 messages from Sarah containing 'deadline': 1) 'The deadline is Friday' - 2 days ago, 2) 'Can we extend the deadline?' - yesterday, 3) 'Deadline confirmed for next Monday' - 5 hours ago

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first before using any Microsoft Teams tools to get the current schema
Verify connection status shows ACTIVE before running any Teams workflows
Use list operations (TEAMS_LIST, LIST_CHANNELS, LIST_USERS) to resolve IDs rather than guessing formats
Split messages longer than 28KB into multiple parts to avoid 413 errors
Handle 429 throttling responses with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s delays)
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode team IDs, channel IDs, or user IDs - always resolve them from list operations first
Do not search for newly posted messages immediately - wait 30-60 seconds for indexing
Do not create meetings with end_date_time before start_date_time - this will fail validation
Do not assume all teams and channels are accessible - private channels require specific permissions

faq.question: Do I need a Microsoft account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Microsoft account with Teams access and must complete OAuth authentication through Rube MCP before using any Teams operations.

faq.question: What is Rube MCP and why is it required?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a service that provides access to Composio's Microsoft Teams toolkit. It acts as a bridge between Claude Code and the Microsoft Graph API. Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server to use this skill.

faq.question: Why did my message fail to send?
faq.answer: Common causes include: incorrect team_id or channel_id format, message larger than 28KB, insufficient permissions (403 error), or API throttling (429 error). Verify IDs from list operations and check error details.

faq.question: Can I create meetings that appear on my calendar?
faq.answer: No, this skill creates standalone Teams meetings. For calendar-linked meetings, you would need to use Outlook Calendar tools with is_online_meeting=true parameter.

faq.question: How do I find the correct team and channel IDs?
faq.answer: Use the MICROSOFT_TEAMS_TEAMS_LIST tool to list all teams, then MICROSOFT_TEAMS_TEAMS_LIST_CHANNELS with the team_id to list channels. Extract the IDs from the responses rather than guessing their format.

faq.question: Why can't I find messages I just posted?
faq.answer: Teams search has a 30-60 second indexing delay. Newly posted messages will not appear in search results immediately. Use message listing tools for real-time verification instead.
